channel.title: CrossWire
channel.description: Twice-daily Hermes Agent ecosystem intelligence — releases, community builds, trending issues, articles, and tutorials.

item.title: CrossWire: Hermes — v0.19.0 "Quicksilver" Release Radar, Training Camp Eve, OpenRouter Code Actions
item.description:
CrossWire: Hermes — Release Radar & Ecosystem

Thursday, July 23, 2026 · Evening Brief

🪐 v0.19.0 "Quicksilver" — Release Radar Deep Dive

Hermes Agent v0.19.0 shipped July 20 with the codename "Quicksilver" — and the numbers are staggering: ~2,245 commits, ~1,065 merged PRs, ~300,000 insertions, ~3,300 issues closed, 450+ community contributors since v0.18.0. This is the biggest single release window in the project's history.

What landed (headline features):

  • ~80% faster cold starts on every platform — first-turn time-to-first-token cut dramatically. Reasoning streams live by default now, so you see the agent working instead of staring at a frozen screen.
  • Desktop app speed overhaul (~20 PRs): 14× faster streaming markdown rendering, virtualized diffs for large files, snappy session switching, incremental TUI markdown rendering.
  • Smart approvals — granular permission controls so you don't have to approve every single tool call.
  • Delivery-obligation ledger — the agent tracks what it promised to do and follows up. This is a big deal for reliability in multi-step workflows.
  • Subagent live transcripts — background agents stream their reasoning in real-time so you can monitor work without polling.
  • Bitwarden & 1Password integration — credentials go through your password manager instead of scattering secrets across config files.
  • /topup command — manage your Nous subscription plan and add credits without leaving the terminal.

MOCR relevance: You're already on v0.19 — no action needed. But the delivery-obligation ledger and subagent live transcripts are directly relevant. The ledger pattern would pair well with MOCR's flight-recorder logging for cross-agent accountability.

BoxminingAI's breakdown video covers Quicksilver speed improvements, 1Password support, background work, and agent transcripts. Also: Hermes v0.19 Quicksilver Update Makes Agents 10X Better from another creator.

Release Patch Chain: v0.18.1 → v0.18.2

Two patch releases on the 0.18.x branch preceded Quicksilver:

  • v0.18.1 (July 7) — Rolled up ~660 PRs from the 6 days since v0.18.0: installer/updater self-healing on Windows, dashboard and gateway fixes, WhatsApp dashboard pairing, MCP and provider fixes. Infrastructure-driven patch tag, not a curated release.
  • v0.18.2 (July 7, same-day) — Same-day patch unpinning the WhatsApp Baileys dependency from a git commit to the published 7.0.0-rc13 npm release. Fixed Docker image builds.

🔥 Trending PRs & Issues

  • #70213: Configurable background review iteration budget (type/feature, P3) — Makes auxiliary.background_review.max_iterations configurable, preserving the default of 16. Open and needs decision. MOCR relevance: Directly affects background-review agent behavior — if MOCR ever uses background review cycles, being able to tune the budget matters.
  • #70260: Derive display versions from release tags (type/feature) — Replaces CalVer git-description with SemVer-derived version boundaries across CLI, TUI, Desktop, and Nix. Exact releases show 0.x.y, later commits show 0.x.y+<distance>. Cleaner version provenance everywhere.
  • #69141: Never auto-delete checkpoints on unattended startup (P0 bug) — Hardens the startup prune so a missing workdir can never silently destroy checkpoint history. delete_orphans=False hardcoded on both startup call sites. MOCR relevance: Critical — unattended startup is exactly MOCR's cron context. This prevents a startup race with an unmounted filesystem from nuking your session history.
  • #50057: Skill Graph — bundled plugin, config, companion skills — Adds a skill_graph_mode config and companion skills. Large feature still open since April 30. Worth watching for when MOCR's skill orchestration needs evolve.
  • #70259: Desktop test coverage for interrupted turns and compression queueing — Testing hardening for desktop session reliability. Good sign the team is investing in edge-case robustness.

New Community Builds (This Week)

  • design-judge-skills (⭐288, +13 since morning) — Evidence-driven Agent Skills for design award research, evaluation, award matching, entry writing, and submission readiness. Gained another 13 stars through the day. The fastest-rising new repo this cycle.
  • frakio-work (⭐50) — Multi-agent workbench stitching Codex + Hermes + Arc. Chinese-language description calls it a "super-stitched workstation centered on multi-agent collaboration." Steady at 50 stars.
  • hermes-improvements (⭐8) — Vector memory, adaptive soul, workflow classification for Hermes. Architecture patterns directly relevant to MOCR's memory-layer roadmap.
  • agent-console (⭐3) — Lightweight browser mission control: chat, sessions, cron, skills, voice, read-only missions. An alternative to the desktop app — lightweight web-based surface for Hermes. MOCR relevance: A browser-based mission control concept directly parallels MOCR's role.
  • slashstack-skills (⭐3) — 11 MIT-licensed skills: /preflight, /audit, /ship, /remember. Plain-markdown agent guardrails for any LLM tool stack.
  • loremaster (⭐2) — AI scrum-master / PM skill pack: living project wiki ("lore vault"), human-approval gates, conflict detection. Directly adjacent to MOCR's operating model.
  • fruvisi (⭐2) — Visual org-chart dashboard: areas & groups, team lineups, per-agent OpenRouter model routing.
  • hermes-newton-operations (⭐2) — Profile-scoped operations dashboard and bounded decision journal for Hermes.

📈 Gaining Popularity (Month-over-Month, 623 repos tracked)

  • hermes-browser-extension (⭐1,096) — Browser-native side panel. Still the #1 community project, crossed 1,000 stars this month.
  • Hermex (⭐906) — Native iPhone app for Hermes. Three weeks old, closing in on 1,000 stars fast.
  • recon-skills (⭐834) — 162 offensive security skills for recon and pentest. Field-validated from 600+ targets across 45+ sectors.
  • design-judge-skills (⭐288) — New entry this week, already top-4.
  • EvoForge (⭐101) — Desktop agent workbench with evolutionary capabilities.
  • memmy-agent (⭐69) — One agent, one memory shared by every AI.
  • mind (⭐58) — Persistent memory for coding agents: auto-capture, explainable recall, portable offline storage.

📖 Worth Reading / Watching

  • BoxminingAI: Hermes Agent v0.19 Breakdown (Quicksilver Release) — 9-min walkthrough of speed improvements, 1Password, smart approvals, background agents. Practical update guide included.
  • Alex Finn: 100 hours of Hermes Agent lessons in 19 minutes — Community veteran's hard-won experience. Worth the time.
  • Tina Huang: Hermes Agent Fundamentals In 29 Minutes — Solid intro if you ever onboard someone to MOCR.
  • Leon van Zyl: Hermes Agent From Setup to 24/7 AI Assistant — Complete guide covering VPS-based 24/7 operation.

CrossWire: Tech — Apple, AI, and AI Spending

Thursday, July 23, 2026 · Evening Brief

🏥 ChatGPT Apple Health Integration Now Rolling Out

As of today, OpenAI's "Health in ChatGPT" feature is rolling out to U.S. users. The integration connects Apple Health data (and MyFitnessPal, etc.) into ChatGPT for personalized health insights, trend analysis, and wellness coaching. OpenAI's help doc confirms it requires iOS and is available through the ChatGPT app's Tools menu or Settings. The gadget hacks analysis calls it a "game-changing 2026 integration."

Worth noting: OpenAI's release notes also confirm Codex remains unchanged in this update. Quick Chats still available.

⚠️ ChatGPT & Codex Partial Outage Today

9to5Mac reports and StatusGator confirms that some users are encountering elevated error rates on both ChatGPT and Codex today. OpenAI's status page acknowledges the issue. The Apple Health rollout and an outage on the same day — classic launch-day jitters.

🎙️ ChatGPT Desktop Gets GPT Voice Mode

OpenAI is updating the ChatGPT desktop app with GPT Voice — a voice mode for talking through work to complete tasks. Voice-as-interface for the desktop workflow, not just chat.

💰 Google's First Negative Cash Flow Quarter — AI Spending Spikes

Ars Technica reports Google just posted its first negative cash flow quarter in history. Capital expenditures hit $36B in Q1 alone, with CFO Anat Ashkenazi guiding 2026 total CapEx to $195B–$205B (up from prior $180B–$190B guidance). The Financial Times reports Google "burned through $6B in cash" as AI spending climbs. BBC's coverage frames it as "Google burning through cash with spiralling AI costs."

Google also hit with $1B in EU Digital Markets Act fines — third major tech giant to face DMA penalties as the EU braces for a Trump trade battle.

📊 Geekbench 7 Launches

Primate Labs released Geekbench 7 today with redesigned multi-core and GPU benchmarks. The first major benchmark suite overhaul in years — CPU and GPU performance metrics get a new baseline.

📱 iPhone Ultra to Account for 25% of Foldable Shipments

Counterpoint Research forecasts 21% YoY growth in global foldable smartphone shipments in 2026, with the rumored iPhone Ultra expected to capture 25% of that market. The foldable iPhone narrative is firming up — Samsung's creaseless display tech + Apple's supply chain scale. 9to5Mac also has three reasons to consider the iPhone 18 Pro upgrade from the 17 Pro.

📡 Emergency SOS via Satellite: Andorra & Iceland

Two more countries added — Apple's satellite SOS coverage continues expanding.

🎮 Sony Kills the Game Disc (Almost)

Ars Technica analyzes Circana data showing US gamers overwhelmingly voted with their digital wallets long before Sony pulled the disc drive. Inevitability, not surprise.

🗓️ Other Tech Headlines

  • Final Coyote vs. Acme trailer drops at SDCC — Plus the full trailer for Zach Cregger's Resident Evil features a "likable everyman."
  • "The Morning Show" returning for final season next year on Apple TV+.
  • Apple Pay: $20 off $120+ at Columbia — Current promo for sportswear.
  • Home Assistant: RSS feed returned empty this cycle — no new HA news to report.

CrossWire: Commanders — Training Camp Eve

Thursday, July 23, 2026 · Evening Brief

🚨 ROOKIES REPORT TOMORROW

Commanders rookies report July 24 — that's tomorrow. Veterans report July 28. Football is back.

11 open practices run August 1–19 in Ashburn, VA. Free tickets, 8:30 AM starts, gates at 7:30 AM. Max 6 tickets + 1 parking pass per day.

Commanders.com's camp overview previews every position group — the tight end preview is up, and the roster breakdown series is running daily.

📋 Pre-Camp Storylines

  • Ground game emphasis: "GMFB" identified the #1 burning question: Can Washington get the ground attack more involved? Jacory Croskey-Merritt and Rachaad White will be camp battles to watch.
  • Top 10 roster: Command Center podcast (Brian Mitchell, Santana Moss, Fred Smoot) ranked their top 10 players on the roster — useful barometer for who's expected to lead.
  • New uniforms: The fully redesigned 2026 jersey schedule was announced — we now know which uniforms will be worn each week.
  • John Riggins' No. 44: Retirement ceremony at halftime of the Week 9 Rams game (Nov 8). Hall of Famer, Super Bowl XVII MVP, "I'm loose, I'm loose" — long overdue.

📅 2026 Schedule

17 games remaining. Season opens Sep 13 @ Eagles. Primetime: Oct 20 (Mon) @ 49ers.

  • Sep 13 — @ Philadelphia Eagles
  • Sep 20 — @ Dallas Cowboys
  • Sep 27 — vs Seattle Seahawks
  • Oct 4 — vs Indianapolis Colts
  • Oct 11 — vs New York Giants
  • Oct 20 (Mon) — @ San Francisco 49ers
  • Nov 2 — vs Philadelphia Eagles
  • Nov 8 — vs Los Angeles Rams (Riggins' No. 44 retirement)
  • Nov 13 — @ New York Giants
  • Nov 24 — vs Cincinnati Bengals
  • Nov 29 — @ Arizona Cardinals
  • Dec 6 — @ Tennessee Titans
  • Dec 13 — vs Houston Texans
  • Dec 20 — vs Atlanta Falcons
  • Dec 27 — @ Minnesota Vikings
  • Jan 3 — @ Jacksonville Jaguars
  • Jan 10 — vs Dallas Cowboys

🏈 NFL Headlines

  • Raiders sign Fernando Mendoza — The No. 1 overall pick signed a 4-year, $57.27M fully guaranteed deal with a fifth-year option. Schefter: Kirk Cousins the favorite to start over Mendoza for Week 1.
  • Ravens waive Diego Pavia — Making room for veteran center Ethan Pocic's signed free agent contract.
  • Former Jets Feely vs. Shah in Arizona election — Jay Feely (former kicker) vs. his former team doctor Amish Shah for Arizona's 1st Congressional District. Unusual political matchup.
  • Fowler: No market for Tyreek Hill — Minimal trade interest as his knee recovery concerns linger. Andrew Hawkins lays out concerns for Hill's future.
  • Chiefs: "Brighter future" — As training camp opens, Kansas City veterans vow to keep the disappointment of 2025 in mind.
  • Fantasy: Jared Goff as a reliable QB2? — Matt Bowen breaks down why Goff could be a consistent QB2 all season.
